page: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 40 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing 40 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor configuration includes a 32 KB L1 data cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and SHA-NI instructions.

The instance is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD, which provides high-speed local data access without requiring external block storage attachments. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In synthetic benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test recorded a score of 3083.57, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng reached 123693.48, positioning the instance in the top 25%. These metrics indicate high computational throughput for parallelized workloads, though the shared CPU allocation model introduces potential performance variability depending on co-tenant activity.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of 40 cores, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of NVMe storage on a shared CPU model offers a high density of resources at a lower price point than dedicated-core equivalents. This makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and batch processing pipelines that require substantial memory and storage but can tolerate the minor CPU scheduling latency associated with shared environments.
description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 40 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the server provides 160.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 3083.57, placing it in the top 10%, and a multi-core score of 123693.48, ranking in the top 25%. The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective alternative to dedicated hardware for resource-dense workloads. This configuration is suitable for database hosting, multi-threaded batch processing, and general-purpose web serving where high memory and local storage capacities are required but minor CPU scheduling latency is acceptable.
